title: Out-of-Range SCADA Setpoints and Invalid Control Commands

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 3.5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 3.5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.30: Out-of-Range SCADA Setpoints and Invalid Control Commands.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.30' running on index=scada sourcetype="hist:control", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=scada sourcetype="hist:control" earliest=-24h
| lookup setpoint_limits.csv tag OUTPUT min_sp, max_sp, allowed_modes
| eval bad=if(value<min_sp OR value>max_sp OR !mvfind(allowed_modes, mode),1,0)
| where bad=1
| table _time, tag, value, min_sp, max_sp, mode, operator
description: Validates control writes against engineering limits and recipe tables to catch dangerous setpoint or mode commands.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes scada and sourcetypes hist:control. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Publish limits from P&ID digital twin; (2) test alarms in shadow mode; (3) integrate with MES recipes; (4) alert operations supervisor. Indexes required: `index=scada`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hist:control`; Lookups: `setpoint_limits`, `setpoint_limits.csv`.
visualization: Time chart, Table, Single value (bad commands).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for validates control writes against engineering limits and recipe tables to catch dangerous setpoint or mode commands. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Historian HEC, DCS export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=scada` `sourcetype="hist:control"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Publish limits from P&ID digital twin; (2) test alarms in shadow mode; (3) integrate with MES recipes; (4) alert operations supervisor.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=scada sourcetype="hist:control" earliest=-24h
| lookup setpoint_limits.csv tag OUTPUT min_sp, max_sp, allowed_modes
| eval bad=if(value<min_sp OR value>max_sp OR !mvfind(allowed_modes, mode),1,0)
| where bad=1
| table _time, tag, value, min_sp, max_sp, mode, operator
```

Understanding this SPL

**Out-of-Range SCADA Setpoints and Invalid Control Commands (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.5)** — Validates control writes against engineering limits and recipe tables to catch dangerous setpoint or mode commands.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=scada` `sourcetype="hist:control"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Historian HEC, DCS export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: scada; **sourcetype**: hist:control. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=scada, sourcetype="hist:control", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bad** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where bad=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, bad, max_sp, min_sp, mode, operator, tag, value.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value (bad commands).
